steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The journalist claims that quality signals like Oscar nominations can drive strong lifetime earnings even from a modest opening weekend. The correct choice needs data showing: an Oscar-nominated film with a small opening weekend that outearned a non-nominated film with a larger opening weekend.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Cinema Paradiso (Oscar) vs. Instructions Not Included (no Oscar): the non-nominated film had higher lifetime earnings — contradicts the claim. ✗
  • Choice B — La Cage aux Folles (Oscar, small opening) vs. Padmaavat (no Oscar, larger opening): the Oscar-nominated film had a smaller opening weekend ($18,709 vs. $4,493,384) but higher lifetime earnings ($20,424,259 vs. $11,846,060) — directly supports the claim. ✓
  • Choice C — La Cage aux Folles vs. Cinema Paradiso: both were Oscar-nominated; comparing two nominated films doesn't test the claim about nominations driving earnings. ✗
  • Choice D — Instructions Not Included vs. Padmaavat: neither was Oscar-nominated; irrelevant to the journalist's claim. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
